name: Mint-Chocolate Truffles (Lowfat)
recipeInstructions:
1. Place 1/3 cup of morsels in a medium glass bowl and microwave at high heat for 1 minute or until the morsels are almost melted. Stir until smooth. Let cool.
2. Add the softened cream cheese to the melted morsels, and beat with a mixer at medium speed until smooth.
3. Add 16 oz. of powdered sugar to the cheese mixture; beat until well-blended.
4. Press the mixture into a 6-inch square on heavy-duty plastic wrap and cover with additional plastic wrap. Chill for at least 1 hour.
5. Remove the top sheet of plastic wrap and cut the mixture into 48 squares. Roll each square into a ball, and place on wax paper.
6. Roll half of the balls in cocoa and the remaining balls in sifted powdered sugar.
7. Place 2 tablespoons of morsels in a heavy-duty zip-top plastic bag and microwave at high heat for 1 minute or until the morsels are softened. Knead the bag until smooth.
8. Snip a tiny hole in the corner of the bag and drizzle chocolate over the balls rolled in cocoa. Serve at room temperature.
Note: Store truffles in a single layer in an airtight container in the freezer for up to 1 month. Let stand at room temperature for 1 hour before serving.
